events.title: Tanker Struck by Drone off Oman
events.description: A Panama-flagged tanker was struck by a drone off the coast of Oman on June 27, amid rising tensions with Iran, which has claimed to be firing warning shots in the region.

events.title: Aluminum cargo released from Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Vitol Group has successfully shipped a stranded cargo of aluminum out of the Strait of Hormuz, indicating potential supply relief for buyers after a period of disruption.

events.title: Saudi Arabia ramps up oil exports as Gulf ports reopen
events.description: Saudi Arabia is increasing crude oil exports as its Persian Gulf ports, previously closed due to the Iran war, resume operations. The reopening is helping restore regional freight flows.

events.title: Tanker Struck in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A tanker was struck in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting naval authorities to raise the threat level for ships in the region, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Second Tanker Struck in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A second merchant vessel has been struck in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting escalating security risks and disrupting critical global shipping routes between the Persian Gulf and the Arabian Sea.

events.title: Container Rates Near Two-Year High
events.description: Containerized freight rates are approaching a two-year high due to renewed concerns over potential tariffs, disrupting global shipping expectations for 2026.

events.title: US-Iran Strait of Hormuz Agreement Extension
events.description: The US and Iran signed a 14-point memorandum extending a 60-day agreement to de-escalate tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: RCL joins Far East–Red Sea container service
events.description: Regional Container Lines (RCL) has joined the Far East–Red Sea RCS/RCX service operated by Global Feeder Shipping and T.S. Lines, marketing it under the name RCR01. The service deploys seven vessels and operates between major Chinese ports and the Red Sea.

events.title: Hormuz Strait faces asymmetric threats
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is experiencing heightened tension as asymmetric threats, including sea mines and unmanned systems, are being deployed, impacting global shipping and energy flows.

events.title: U.S. Airstrike on Iran Following Strait of Hormuz Attack
events.description: U.S. forces conducted an airstrike on Iranian territory in retaliation for an Iranian attack on a Taiwanese container ship, the Ever Lovely, in the Strait of Hormuz. The incident raises concerns about regional stability and potential disruptions to global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: ZIM revises ZMP service, adds Tianjin
events.description: ZIM Integrated Shipping Services has updated its Mediterranean–Far East ZMP service to include a call at Tianjin, removing Busan from the rotation. Busan will now be served via transshipment at Ningbo.

events.title: Transpacific container rates rise 12% weekly
events.description: Container spot rates on the transpacific Asia-North America west coast trade increased by 12% week-on-week, with the Shanghai-Los Angeles leg reaching $5,750 per 40ft container, 54% higher than the same week in 2025.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic normalizes
events.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is normalizing, reducing concerns over Middle East supply risks and easing pressure on European natural gas prices.

events.title: Surging Hormuz Shipments Restore Exports
events.description: Shipping transits through the Strait of Hormuz have accelerated, restoring Persian Gulf crude oil exports to about 75% of prewar levels following progress toward a US-Iran peace deal.

events.title: Houston Bunker Demand Declines
events.description: Bunker fuel demand in the port of Houston has softened, particularly for VLSFO, according to a trader. Recommended lead times for VLSFO and HSFO are now 6-7 days.

events.title: Cargo ship attacked near Oman
events.description: A cargo ship was attacked near Oman, raising concerns about regional security, though easing traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and U.S.-Iran peace optimism have offset some fears.

events.title: War-driven decline in tanker flows
events.description: Tanker flows dropped sharply in late March due to wartime disruptions, reaching a low of 3.31M barrels on 21 May before recovering to 6.97M barrels by 24 June.

events.title: Hormuz Strait maritime traffic resumes
events.description: Maritime traffic through the Hormuz Strait is expected to resume following a disruption, impacting global energy markets and supply chain flows for oil and gas.

events.title: Iranian Attack Adds Uncertainty to Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is seeing increased oil traffic, but an Iranian attack has introduced uncertainty and potential disruption to this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: SC Ports suspends Leatherman Terminal operations
events.description: South Carolina Ports will temporarily pause container operations at Leatherman Terminal starting August to address lower cargo volumes and an uncertain trade outlook. Operations will shift to Wando and North Charleston terminals.

events.title: Bourbon shipment stolen via carrier impersonation
events.description: A $500,000 shipment of Noble Oak Bourbon was stolen when suspects impersonated authorized carriers to pick up the freight. The incident highlights a growing cargo theft tactic involving impersonation and legitimate shipment information.

events.title: Iran accused of drone attack on ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating a U.S. ceasefire by conducting a drone attack on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Oman Considers Fees for Hormuz Strait Transit
events.description: Oman has informed European officials that the pre-war status quo for the Strait of Hormuz cannot be restored, and ships transiting the strait may be required to pay fees, signaling a potential policy shift affecting global shipping.

events.title: Iran reasserts control over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran reasserted its right to control shipping in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a ship near Oman, raising concerns about regional stability and maritime security.

events.title: 80 Mines Estimated in Hormuz Shipping Lanes
events.description: The International Maritime Organization estimates there are approximately 80 mines in the Strait of Hormuz's shipping lanes, significantly complicating the return of normal operations in this critical oil transport channel.

events.title: Yemen tensions threaten Red Sea shipping
events.description: Rising tensions between the Houthis and the Internationally Recognized Government in Yemen are causing concern for Red Sea shipping routes, with potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: Increase in containers lost at sea in 2025
events.description: The World Shipping Council reported 1,478 containers were lost at sea in 2025, up from 576 in 2024. While representing just 0.0005% of global container traffic, the increase highlights ongoing vessel-related disruptions in global freight flows.

events.title: South Carolina Ports suspends container terminal operations
events.description: South Carolina Ports has temporarily shut down a key container terminal due to trade uncertainty and cost-cutting measures, disrupting freight operations at the Port of Charleston.

events.title: Drone Strike on Ever Lovely in Strait
events.description: The Singapore-flagged containership Ever Lovely was struck by an unknown projectile on its starboard side on 25 June 2026, raising concerns about security and safety in the Strait of Malacca.

events.title: Lufthansa Cargo upgrades Frankfurt hub
events.description: Lufthansa Cargo has launched the first phase of a €600 million upgrade to its cargo hub at Frankfurt Airport, aiming to modernize operations and increase efficiency.

events.title: Transpacific spot freight rates hit 22-month high
events.description: Spot freight rates for 40ft containers from Shanghai to Los Angeles rose 12% to $5,750, contributing to a 5% weekly increase in the Drewry World Container Index to $4,166, its highest in 22 months.

events.title: Box ship attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The containership Ever Lovely was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to paused evacuation operations and conflicting claims about the vessel's transit status. The IMO has called for safety guarantees for vessels in the area.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic slows after ship attack
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz slowed following an attack on a Taiwanese-operated ship by Iran, according to ship tracking data. Fewer vessels transited the strait on Friday compared to earlier in the week.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Boosts Panama Canal Traffic
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has driven increased ship traffic through the Panama Canal, leading to higher-than-expected revenue forecasts for the canal in fiscal 2026.

events.title: EU extends economic sanctions against Russia
events.description: The European Council has extended economic sanctions against Russia for another year, until 31 July 2027, in response to the ongoing war in Ukraine. This policy move is likely to continue affecting global supply chains, particularly in energy, freight, and trade flows.

events.title: Vessel hit in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A commercial vessel was struck in the Strait of Hormuz, causing a temporary halt in oil market sell-off and highlighting ongoing risks in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz evacuation paused after cargo vessel attack
events.description: The International Maritime Organization has suspended its evacuation operation in the Strait of Hormuz following an attack on a cargo vessel in the Gulf of Oman, raising concerns about maritime security in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz vessel traffic surges 48%
events.description: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz rose sharply this week, with 31 confirmed passages by mid-morning, representing a 48% increase from the previous day, according to Windward.

events.title: UK detains Russia-linked oil tanker Smyrtos
events.description: The 106,800 dwt Russia-linked oil tanker Smyrtos was seized off southern England by UK forces, marking the first UK-led interdiction of a shadow fleet tanker. Russia has warned of legal action if the cargo is sold, escalating tensions around enforcement of sanctions.

events.title: Evergreen containership hit near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An Evergreen containership was struck shortly after transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing the International Maritime Organization to pause its evacuation operation for vessels in the Gulf. The incident raises concerns about ongoing disruptions in this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Evergreen container ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The 9,532 teu Evergreen containership Ever Lovely was struck shortly after transiting the Strait of Hormuz, causing the International Maritime Organization to pause its evacuation operation for vessels in the Gulf.

events.title: France intercepts Russian shadow fleet tanker near Sicily
events.description: French navy boarded the Russia-linked tanker Deliver near Sicily as part of efforts to disrupt Moscow's shadow fleet circumventing Western sanctions. The incident highlights ongoing disruptions in global energy freight flows.

events.title: Shipyard engine shortages curb output
events.description: A shortage of main engines, particularly dual-fuel low-speed two-stroke engines, is limiting shipyard output, echoing constraints from the 2007 shipbuilding boom. This shortage is creating bottlenecks in the shipbuilding supply chain.

events.title: Charleston suspends new terminal operations due to low volume
events.description: South Carolina Ports has paused operations at its new terminal in Charleston, citing low cargo volumes and high operating costs. The decision may impact regional port efficiency and freight handling capacity.

events.title: Containers Lost Overboard Rise in 2024
events.description: The number of containers lost overboard increased for the second consecutive year, with incidents contributing to the fourth-highest level on record, raising concerns about maritime safety and cargo loss.

events.title: KONGSBERG upgrades Great Belt VTS traffic system
events.description: KONGSBERG has delivered a new Vessel Traffic Services (VTS) system for the Great Belt in Denmark, enhancing traffic management through one of Europe's busiest waterways.

events.title: Ship Attack Off Oman Disrupts IMO Evacuation
events.description: A merchant vessel was attacked in the Gulf of Oman, leading the International Maritime Organization to suspend its coordinated evacuation of ships stranded in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: KONGSBERG upgrades Great Belt VTS traffic management
events.description: KONGSBERG has delivered an advanced Vessel Traffic Services solution for Great Belt VTS in Denmark, enhancing traffic management through one of Europe’s busiest straits.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes, easing oil supply concerns
events.description: Improved traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has eased concerns over Middle East crude supply disruptions, contributing to a fourth consecutive session of falling oil prices.

events.title: EU Proposes Sanctions on Vessels Aiding Russian Shadow Fleet
events.description: The EU proposed its 21st sanctions package against Russia, including targeting vessels that support the Russian shadow fleet by providing services like bunkering. The sanctions aim to disrupt Russian trade and energy flows.

events.title: Containership attacked near Strait of Hormuz
events.location: South of Oman, near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A containership was struck by a projectile south of Oman near the eastern exit of the Strait of Hormuz. The International Maritime Organization has paused evacuations in the area amid the incident.

events.title: Gas Tanker Collides with Fishing Trawler Off South Korea
events.description: A small gas tanker collided with a fishing trawler off the coast of Ulsan, South Korea, prompting a search and rescue operation by the Ulsan Coast Guard on June 25.

events.title: Charleston Terminal Suspends Operations Due to Low Volume
events.description: South Carolina Ports has suspended operations at its new terminal in Charleston, citing low cargo volumes and high operating costs.

events.title: ILWU strike against California sugar processor
events.description: International Longshore and Warehouse Union workers have initiated the first strike in decades against a major California sugar processor, potentially disrupting freight and logistics operations.

events.title: Attack on cargo vessel in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An unidentified cargo vessel was the target of a suspected attack off Oman as shipping resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Containership Struck Off Oman Coast Amid Hormuz Warnings
events.description: A Singapore-flagged containership was struck by an unknown projectile off the coast of Oman shortly after Iran's IRGC renewed Hormuz Strait transit warnings, raising concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade.

events.title: France Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
events.description: France's navy seized a tanker linked to Russia's 'shadow fleet,' intensifying European efforts to enforce sanctions against Russian oil exports.

events.title: Port Qasim offices closed for Ashura holiday
events.description: Port Qasim Authority offices in Pakistan are closed on June 25-26, 2026, for the Ashura holiday, potentially affecting port operations and administrative processes.

events.title: Iran-Oman Tensions Impact Gulf Shipping Exit Plan
events.description: Tensions between Iran and Oman have emerged as the IMO coordinates a plan to manage the exit of ships trapped in the Gulf, raising concerns about potential disruptions to maritime traffic.

events.title: French Forces Detain Sanctioned Tanker
events.description: French forces detained a sanctioned tanker linked to the shadow fleet off the coast of Sicily on June 23, as part of ongoing enforcement actions against illicit oil trade.

events.title: Port of Long Beach imports surge 40%
events.description: The Port of Long Beach experienced a 40% surge in imports in May, setting a record for cargo growth amid ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty.

events.title: Europe extends Middle East flight restrictions
events.description: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) has renewed its advisory against flying over Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon, forcing European airlines to take longer routes and increasing operating costs, despite improved aviation activity in the Gulf.

events.title: Arctic shipping route interest rises amid risks
events.description: Melting sea ice and geopolitical disruptions are driving interest in Arctic shipping routes between Asia and Europe. However, Allianz Commercial warns of high risks, including sanctions, geopolitical tensions, and environmental challenges.

events.title: Saudi Oil Terminal Resumes Loading After Conflict
events.description: Saudi Arabia is restarting crude oil loading at the Ras Tanura terminal in the Persian Gulf, signaling a resumption of Middle Eastern oil supplies following the Iran war.

events.title: Ships Turn Back in Strait of Hormuz Over IRGC Warnings
events.description: Merchant ships are reportedly turning back in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) renews warnings, raising concerns over potential disruptions to global shipping.

events.title: China-India container rates surge due to strong demand
events.description: Container freight rates from China to India have risen by 25-30% since late May due to strong demand from Indian importers, despite increased capacity. Spot rates from Shanghai to Nhava Sheva are now around $2,300 per TEU.

events.title: IMO unveils Hormuz evacuation plan, vessels transit
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) published a Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan, prompting increased vessel transits through the region. Maersk shipped two vessels through the strait in coordination with security measures.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes, easing oil supply concerns
events.description: Oil prices fell to pre-Iran war levels as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz normalized, reducing concerns over Middle East crude supply disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz MoU and Joint Working Group
events.description: A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) was signed between Iran and the United States, and a Joint Working Group was established between Iran and Oman to improve maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz crisis reshapes maritime governance
events.description: The ongoing Hormuz crisis is altering global maritime governance as Iran's tactics influence shipping behavior, with vessels no longer reverting to previous norms once adjustments are made.

events.title: Maersk Baltimore exits Persian Gulf
events.description: Maersk confirmed that its vessel Maersk Baltimore and a time-chartered vessel have safely exited the Persian Gulf after a successful transit through the Strait of Hormuz following detailed security assessments.

events.title: Severe global shortage of shipping officers by 2030
events.description: The shipping industry faces a critical shortage of over 113,000 STCW-certified officers by 2030, threatening the operation of the global merchant fleet, according to a report by BIMCO and the International Chamber of Shipping.

events.title: Experienced Docker Dies in Mooring Line Incident
events.description: An experienced docker was killed in a mooring line incident at a ferry terminal in Cairnryan, Scotland, raising concerns about safety in port operations.

events.title: IRGC Rejects Omani Safe-Passage Plan for Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy has rejected Oman's proposed safe transit plan for the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about potential disruptions to one of the world's most critical shipping chokepoints.

events.title: IMO evacuation plan for stranded seafarers in Middle East
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has launched an evacuation plan for approximately 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Middle East since late February. The International Chamber of Shipping (ICS) has welcomed the initiative, emphasizing the priority of safely evacuating the affected crew.

events.title: FedEx retires 10 MD-11 cargo jets
events.description: FedEx is retiring 10 aging MD-11 cargo jets while returning others to service after a seven-month grounding following a UPS freighter crash. This fleet adjustment reflects ongoing operational changes in air freight logistics.

events.title: Shadow VLCC Era resurfaces under Comoros flag
events.description: The 2002-built VLCC Era, part of the shadow fleet with a history of identity changes, has resurfaced under the Comoros flag. The vessel, previously known as Marinera and Bella 1, is now being tracked under a new identity, raising questions about its operational status and potential implications for the shadow fleet.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic shows recovery signs
events.description: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is increasing, signaling a gradual return to normal operations after months of disruption, though the waterway remains under strict restrictions and many seafarers are still stranded.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Rising
events.description: Traffic levels in the Strait of Hormuz are increasing as a formal plan for outbound transits takes effect, indicating improved flow after prior disruptions.

events.title: CMA CGM introduces hazardous cargo surcharge on key routes
events.location: Far East to Indian Ocean, South Africa, East Africa
events.description: CMA CGM has announced a new Hazardous Surcharge of US$500 per TEU for hazardous cargo shipped from the Far East to the Indian Ocean, South Africa, and East Africa, effective 1 July 2026.

events.title: Black Sea and Hormuz Maritime Security Escalation
events.location: Black Sea and Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Dryad Global Maritime Intelligence Brief reports increased maritime security risks in the Black Sea and Strait of Hormuz, including drone strikes targeting merchant vessels and port infrastructure, raising concerns for global freight flows.

events.title: Oman and Iran discuss Hormuz Strait administration
events.description: Oman and Iran have initiated discussions on the administrative framework for the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. A working group will be formed to address navigation management and costs, potentially impacting maritime logistics through the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopens, seafarers evacuated
events.description: The International Maritime Organization is evacuating over 11,000 seafarers from the Strait of Hormuz as the shipping corridor reopens following a US-Iran peace agreement aimed at restoring maritime security.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic recovers after Iran peace talks
events.description: Oil prices fell as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz showed signs of recovery following improved U.S.-Iran relations and peace talks, easing concerns over energy supply disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening uncertainty
events.description: Uncertainty remains about the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, affecting container shipping routes and market stability.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transit challenges persist despite peace agreement
events.description: War coverage is available for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, but security and legal issues continue to hinder normal maritime traffic. A preliminary peace agreement between Iran and the US, signed on June 18, aims to restore cargo flows through the region.

events.title: Iran releases crews from seized MSC ship
events.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has released Filipino and Ukrainian crew members from a ship operated by Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) that had been seized. The release follows ongoing tensions in the region affecting maritime operations.

events.title: Containership Fire Due to Misdeclared Cargo
events.description: A containership fire now occurs roughly every 17 days worldwide, primarily due to misdeclared or undeclared dangerous cargo, according to recent data.

events.title: Diplomatic developments in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The global marine insurance industry is cautiously welcoming recent diplomatic developments in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy chokepoint. Uncertainty remains over how the strait will be managed, with Iran reportedly seeking to avoid tolls.

events.title: Oil tanker booked at 897% of benchmark rate in Persian Gulf
events.description: A major supertanker operator has provisionally booked a vessel to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times the benchmark rate, highlighting a severe shortage of available ships in the region.

events.title: Stranded Oil Tankers Exit Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Three stranded oil tankers carrying 5 million barrels of crude oil are exiting the Strait of Hormuz, with two heading to Asia, following an interim Iran-U.S. deal easing supply blockages and lowering global prices.

events.title: UK Minehunters Deploy to Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UK's mine countermeasures force has arrived in the Middle East to support a multinational mission aimed at securing the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Port of Long Beach sees strong cargo growth in May
events.description: The Port of Long Beach handled 842,030 TEUs in May, a 31.7% increase from May 2025, driven by importers moving cargo ahead of potential tariff increases and market uncertainty.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transits rise amid renewed tensions
events.description: Transits through the Strait of Hormuz are increasing following a US-Iran interim agreement, though Iran has announced a renewed closure amid Israel-Hezbollah clashes. The situation remains volatile, with transits still below pre-war levels.

events.title: IMO Orders Ships to Halt Movement in Hormuz Amid Evacuation
events.description: The International Maritime Organization is directing ships in the Persian Gulf to remain stationary as it initiates the evacuation of thousands of seafarers affected by ongoing disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic recovers after Iran peace talks
events.description: Oil prices fell as the Strait of Hormuz gradually reopened following improved U.S.-Iran relations, easing concerns over energy supply disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Evacuation Plan Announced
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) announced an evacuation plan in the Strait of Hormuz following a Memorandum of Understanding between Iran and the United States. The agreement aims to ease tensions and address the hardship faced by seafarers and global trade.

events.title: Rio Brasil Terminal adds quay cranes
events.description: Rio Brasil Terminal, operated by ICTSI, has added two new quay cranes as part of its expansion and modernisation programme. The terminal now has six cranes capable of handling vessels up to 400 metres in length.

events.title: TVL re-enters liner shipping with Hong Kong-Taiwan service
events.description: Taiwanese forwarder TVL Marine is re-entering container shipping with a new Hong Kong-Taiwan shuttle service, using five 1,100-1,800 teu ships previously chartered to KMTC Line.

events.title: Bunker Fuel Supply Delays in Singapore and Malaysia
events.description: Bunker fuel availability in Singapore and Malaysia is experiencing significant delays, with VLSFO suppliers recommending lead times ranging from nine days to over three weeks. This disruption could impact vessel operations and fuel logistics in the region.

events.title: IMO evacuates 11,000 stranded seafarers in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has launched an evacuation plan for over 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz following a peace agreement between Iran and the United States. The operation involves cooperation with Iran, Oman, the U.S., and other coastal states.

events.title: Medlog boosts Dhaka terminal with low-cost barge model
events.description: MSC's Medlog has revitalized Bangladesh's underused Pangaon Inland Container Terminal by offering lower transport costs and reducing reliance on congested highways. The terminal, near Dhaka, is attracting more importers and exporters, potentially improving regional freight efficiency.

events.title: US Customs enforcement crackdown
events.description: The US government is tightening customs enforcement with revised regulations and higher penalties for importers and brokers, creating operational concerns and potential delays for supply chain operations.

events.title: Norway offshore labour strike escalates
events.description: A strike by Norwegian Union of Energy Workers (SAFE) has begun disrupting offshore drilling and well operations, with employers preparing a potential lockout. The industrial action started on 15 June over a collective wage agreement dispute.

events.title: Hormuz Strait phased reopening
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz are gradually resuming following a phased reopening coordinated by the IMO and Oman, with international efforts to clear stranded ships in a controlled manner.

events.title: KCCI rises 11.88% due to stronger container freight rates
events.description: The Korea Ocean Business Corporation's Container Composite Index (KCCI) increased 11.88% week-on-week to 3,747 points, driven by higher container export spot rates from Busan on major international trade routes, including significant rises on U.S. West Coast and other key routes.

events.title: Ships return to Hormuz Strait central corridor
events.description: The first ships have returned to the central corridor of the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a slow return to normalcy after the war began. However, tanker owners and operators remain cautious.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz conflict disrupts oil supply
events.description: Conflict in the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted oil supply, causing sharp increases in shipping fuel prices and pushing the industry toward alternative fuels.

events.title: Mass evacuation of seafarers trapped in Persian Gulf
events.description: The International Maritime Organization is launching a large-scale evacuation of over 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf following a recent U.S.-Iran peace agreement. The situation highlights a significant disruption to maritime operations in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Phased Evacuation Launched
events.description: The IMO and Oman have initiated a 'phased evacuation' for transits through the Strait of Hormuz, following diplomatic progress between the U.S. and Iran. The move aims to manage traffic and ensure safe passage amid ongoing tensions in the region.

events.title: Dismantling of beached MSC Baltic III in Canada
events.description: Dismantling operations are underway for the beached containership MSC Baltic III in Canada, according to the Canadian Coast Guard. The incident poses a significant vessel-related disruption to local maritime operations.

events.title: MSC updates FAK rates for South Asia to Europe
events.description: MSC has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for cargo moving from South Asia to Europe, effective from July 1, 2026. The updated pricing applies to shipments from Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan to European gateways including Antwerp and Valencia.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure risk remains despite ceasefire
events.description: A US-Iran memorandum promises the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but actual reopening has not occurred, leaving the critical chokepoint at risk of continued closure and disrupting global freight flows.

events.title: US revokes 20,000 Mexican trucker visas
events.description: The U.S. has revoked 20,000 work visas for Mexican truck drivers since April 2025 as part of an expanded crackdown on cabotage, potentially disrupting cross-border freight operations.

events.title: Iran asserts control over Persian Gulf gateway
events.description: Iran is likely to assert control over the gateway to the Persian Gulf, raising concerns about disruptions to crude oil shipments and regional freight flows.

events.title: Iran and Oman Discuss Hormuz Strait Governance
events.description: Iran and Oman have begun formal talks on the future administration of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, including maritime services and associated costs, raising concerns about potential disruptions to this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Suez Canal traffic up amid Hormuz rerouting
events.description: Suez Canal traffic is increasing as cargo reroutes away from the Hormuz Strait due to geopolitical risks, while Egypt's economic growth forecast declines, highlighting a disconnect between shipping activity and fiscal stability.

events.title: Gulf carriers boost airfreight recovery amid high rates
events.description: Gulf carriers are helping to restore airfreight capacity following disruptions from the US-Iran conflict, but global air cargo rates remain elevated despite easing tensions and falling fuel prices.

events.title: Shipping resumes cautiously through Hormuz Strait
events.description: A trickle of container ships is cautiously resuming transit through the Hormuz Strait following a 60-day MoU between the US and Iran. Vessels are navigating mined waters amid ongoing peace talks in Switzerland.

events.title: Increased Tanker Traffic Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: More tankers are broadcasting their crossings through the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a rise in confidence among shipowners and traders as tensions in the region appear to ease.

events.title: KGTL Port Invests $100M Amid Iran War Cargo Surge
events.description: Pakistan's Karachi Gateway Terminal is planning up to $100 million in new investment over five years due to a cargo surge linked to the Iran war, aiming to reduce freight costs and boost regional shipping.

events.title: China tightens rare earths export rules
events.description: China has placed 10 U.S. companies under stricter rare earths export restrictions, potentially disrupting trade flows and increasing pressure to develop non-Chinese supply chains.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic resuming amid conflicting guidance
events.description: Commercial shipping is resuming through the Strait of Hormuz, but U.S. and Iranian forces are providing conflicting guidance on the safest route, creating uncertainty for freight flows.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: Starting 1 July, the EU will end its de minimis exemption for low-value imports, requiring customs duties on parcels valued under €150. Retailers are warned of potential margin pressures and the need to adjust customs processes.

events.title: Port congestion and rate hikes in July
events.description: Shanghai Port is experiencing significant congestion with delays of four to five days, driven by strong demand for solar panels and the early peak season. This has led to 3.4m teu of box ship capacity being stranded, prompting carriers to plan major rate hikes for July.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS on Far East-East Africa/Seychelles routes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) effective 1 July 2026 for shipments from the Far East and China to East Africa and the Seychelles, impacting freight costs on these trade routes.

events.title: K Shipbuilding sale collapse
events.description: The sale of South Korean shipbuilder K Shipbuilding has collapsed after negotiations with the sole bidder failed to reach an agreement on deal terms, potentially impacting shipbuilding capacity and supply chain operations.

events.title: US-Iran Hormuz Communication Channel Established
events.description: The US and Iran have set up a direct communication line to prevent incidents and reduce miscommunication in the Strait of Hormuz, following high-level talks in Switzerland.

events.title: US grants Iran oil sanctions waiver
events.description: The Trump administration issued a temporary sanctions waiver allowing the production, sale, and shipment of Iranian oil, marking a significant shift from the 'maximum pressure' policy.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Mine Risk and Dual Transit Regime
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is open again, but shipping faces challenges due to the presence of mines and a new dual transit regime, complicating the return to normal commercial traffic.

events.title: US-Iran talks ease Hormuz Strait supply fears
events.description: Oil prices fell as progress in US-Iran negotiations eased concerns over potential supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil transit route.

events.title: Explosion at Qatar's key natural gas export terminal
events.description: A major explosion occurred at Qatar's main natural gas export terminal, injuring dozens and leaving 18 people unaccounted for. The incident happened as workers attempted to resume operations following prior damage from an Iranian attack.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz risk framework outdated
events.description: The risk landscape for the Strait of Hormuz has evolved since the 2026 crisis, but compliance frameworks have not kept pace. Vessels still transit the strait, but under changed and heightened risk conditions.

events.title: Russian drones attack ships off Ukraine, killing seafarer
events.description: Russian drones attacked three commercial ships off the coast of Ukraine on June 22, killing an Egyptian seafarer. Ukraine has condemned the attacks, highlighting ongoing disruptions to shipping in the Black Sea.

events.title: U.S. suspends Iranian oil and tanker restrictions
events.description: The U.S. Treasury has temporarily lifted restrictions on Iranian oil and tankers for 60 days, signaling progress in U.S.-Iran negotiations. This policy shift could impact global oil supply chains and tanker operations.

events.title: Gas Plant Blast Injures Dozens in Qatar
events.description: A major blast at the Ras Laffan gas complex in Qatar has killed 13 workers and injured 66 others, raising concerns about industrial safety and potential impacts on energy logistics.

events.title: UPS considers outsourcing UK parcel delivery
events.description: UPS is exploring outsourcing last-mile parcel delivery in the UK to third-party couriers, which could significantly alter labor dynamics and operations for traditional parcel carriers.

events.title: Ocean carriers impose emergency fuel surcharges
events.description: Ocean carriers have introduced emergency bunker surcharges amid the Iran conflict, raising concerns that shippers may be paying double for fuel price increases. This industry move impacts global freight costs and payment structures.

events.title: Port of Virginia deepens harbor to boost container capacity
events.description: The Port of Virginia has completed a $450 million project to deepen its harbor, enhancing its competitiveness as an East Coast container gateway.

events.title: Explosion at Qatar LNG Site During Restart
events.description: Thirteen people were killed and dozens injured in an explosion at Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex during a restart of operations following a March Iranian attack. The incident could disrupt LNG production and export activities.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic resumes after weekend closure
events.description: Oil and LNG tankers resumed transits through the Strait of Hormuz after Iran closed the waterway over the weekend, indicating a partial resumption of traffic following the disruption.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts crude oil supply
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted crude oil and heavy residual material supplies to US Gulf Coast refineries, prompting increased reliance on Venezuelan crude to meet processing needs.

events.title: Iran war causes oil demand destruction and potential overhang
events.description: The Iran war has caused a significant drop in global oil demand, according to the IEA. A resolution could lead to a surge in supply and a major oil overhang in 2024.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz oil flow recovery delayed
events.description: Despite a US-Iran deal, oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz face a prolonged recovery due to infrastructure damage and a backlog of tankers. Analysts warn that full normalization of Middle East oil exports will take time.

events.title: Intra-Asia container rates hit 2023 high
events.description: Drewry’s Intra-Asia Container Index rose 1% to $1,114 per 40ft container, reaching its highest level since May 2023. Rates are more than double pre-conflict levels due to regional chokepoint closures and route diversions.

events.title: MOL expects Hormuz Strait transit delays
events.description: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines (MOL) CEO Jotaro Tamura estimates it could take several weeks to a month for normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz to resume, citing ongoing disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic discrepancy
events.description: The official traffic count for the Strait of Hormuz no longer aligns with market-tracked data, highlighting a growing divergence that impacts global freight flows.

events.title: Noida Airport opens as alternative to Delhi air cargo congestion
events.description: Noida International Airport (NIA) has begun handling cargo flights, offering an alternative to Delhi Airport amid growing congestion. The airport, operated under a 20-year concession by AISATS, aims to ease capacity constraints in the region.

events.title: Air cargo capacity constraints hinder relief for early peak season
events.description: Tight ocean freight capacity and an early retail peak season are pushing shippers toward air cargo, but limited air freight availability is restricting their ability to shift cargo effectively.

events.title: Yang Ming reports full transpacific and Asia-North Europe freight slots
events.description: Yang Ming announced that freight slots on its transpacific and Asia-North Europe routes are fully booked into July, with demand expected to continue into Q3 due to retailer restocking ahead of Christmas and the expiration of US import tariffs.

events.title: Chile's San Antonio Outer Port Expansion Approved
events.description: Chile has approved a $4.45 billion expansion of the San Antonio Outer Port (El Puerto Exterior), marking the country's largest port development. The project aims to modernize and expand port capacity, potentially impacting regional freight flows and logistics infrastructure.

events.title: Hormuz Strait negotiations and renewed tensions
events.description: Negotiations between the US and Iran to stabilize the Strait of Hormuz continue, following a brief reopening after a pre-deal MoU. The strait closed again due to renewed hostilities in Lebanon, disrupting shipping flows.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: Starting 1 July, the EU will end the de minimis exemption for low-value imports, requiring customs duties of €3 on parcels valued under €150. This policy shift is expected to significantly impact ecommerce logistics and create new operational challenges and opportunities for forwarders.

events.title: Mediterranean spot rate premium surges past $1,500
events.description: The price differential between Asia-North Europe and Asia-Mediterranean spot rates has reached a level not seen since 2022, with the Mediterranean premium exceeding $1,500 per 40ft container, according to the 4 June World Container Index.

events.title: China prepares second LNG terminal for Russian Arctic cargoes
events.description: China is setting up a second import terminal to handle liquefied natural gas from Russia's sanctioned Arctic LNG 2 project, expanding its capacity to process these cargoes beyond a single facility.

events.title: Iranian Crude Exports Surge Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has increased crude oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz to the highest level since the war began, as shipping activity rises amid ongoing peace talks between Tehran and Washington.

events.title: Ukrainian attack disrupts Kerch Strait ferry
events.description: Ukraine has attacked the Kerch Strait ferry crossing, causing its shutdown and disrupting Russian fuel supplies. Military truck traffic has been diverted as a result.

events.title: Uncertainty over Hormuz Agreement Stability
events.description: Industry readers express uncertainty about the long-term stability of the Hormuz agreement, indicating ongoing concerns about potential disruptions in this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Nirint removes Halifax from Cuba service route
events.description: Nirint Shipping has removed Halifax from the northbound leg of its Europe–Cuba–Canada multipurpose service, simplifying the route. The service will now skip Halifax in its rotation, impacting freight flows to Canada.

events.title: ANL implements rate restoration on Asia–Australia trade
events.description: ANL has announced a rate restoration programme for shipments from Asia to Australia, effective July 15, 2026. The increase will apply to all dry and refrigerated cargo, added on top of existing Spot and FAK rates.

events.title: Turkish-owned bulker struck by drone in Black Sea
events.description: A Turkish-owned dry bulk vessel was struck by a drone in the Black Sea, causing it to catch fire. The incident underscores heightened risks for commercial shipping amid escalating tensions between Russia and Ukraine.

events.title: Conflicting US-Iran Hormuz Strait Status Reports
events.description: The United States and Iran issued conflicting statements on the status of the Strait of Hormuz, a key energy chokepoint, causing uncertainty and reducing vessel transits below normal levels.

events.title: Iran Shuts Strait of Hormuz, Slows Shipping
events.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz dropped sharply after Iran announced it had again closed the waterway, disrupting a key global shipping route.

events.title: DP World installs large quay cranes at Southampton port
events.description: DP World has received two of Europe's largest quay cranes at Southampton port as part of a £60 million investment to expand capacity. The cranes, each weighing over 2,000 tonnes, arrived fully assembled by sea and will be installed to support operations at the UK's busiest container port.

events.title: Oil Flow Continues Through Strait of Hormuz Despite Iran Closure Claim
events.description: Despite Iran claiming to have shut down the Strait of Hormuz, oil continued to flow through the critical waterway this weekend, highlighting ongoing tensions but no immediate disruption to global oil shipments.

events.title: Tanker Incident Near Al-shihr, Yemen
events.description: A tanker reported being approached by an armed skiff approximately 50 nautical miles southeast of Al-shihr, Yemen, raising concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Transit Security Volatility
events.description: Security in the Strait of Hormuz remains highly unstable, with US efforts enabling only a gradual increase in vessel transit. The situation is described as fluctuating 'hour to hour,' indicating ongoing risk for shipping.

events.title: Russian-linked vessels loitering over European subsea cables
events.description: Russian-flagged and linked vessels have been observed zig-zagging, loitering, and taking uneconomic routes above European subsea cables in the Atlantic, with no commercial justification. This behavior raises concerns about potential interference with critical undersea infrastructure.

events.title: Ardmore expands clean tanker fleet amid Hormuz volatility
events.description: Ardmore Shipping increases its newbuild orderbook to four product and chemical tankers, signaling anticipation of ongoing volatility in tanker markets linked to potential disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopens After MoU Signing
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz reopened for commercial traffic on June 18 following a U.S.-Iran MoU signed on June 17. A total of 18 vessels transited during the initial window, marking a significant development in regional freight flows.

events.title: Hormuz Strait tensions ease amid fragile US-Iran peace deal
events.description: The Hormuz Strait is seeing a tentative return to normalcy following a peace agreement between the US and Iran, though the situation remains fragile. Shipbroker Gibson noted that after over three months of disruption, the conflict appears closer to resolution.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Closure Disrupts Traffic
events.description: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon, causing a significant drop in marine traffic as shipowners comply with the order.

events.title: CMA CGM implements PSS on key trade lanes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced multiple Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) on key trade lanes, including shipments from Asia to Canada, West Africa, the Mediterranean, and North Africa, effective between 22 June and 10 July 2026.

events.title: Maersk extends WAF7 service to South Africa
events.description: Maersk has extended its WAF7 service to include South Africa, adding calls at Port Elizabeth to support the country's fruit export season. The route now connects Mediterranean and West African ports with South Africa.

events.title: New direct rail link launched between Baltic Hub and Ostrava
events.description: Loconi Intermodal has launched a direct weekly rail service from the Baltic Hub in Gdańsk to the PKP Cargo International Terminal in Ostrava, Czech Republic, starting in May 2026. This marks the first direct rail connection between the two terminals.

events.title: MSC launches Sierra service for Far East-Mexico route
events.description: MSC has launched the Sierra service, a new Far East–Mexico route complementing its Mexicas loop. The service operates between Shanghai, Qingdao, Busan, Manzanillo, and Lazaro Cardenas.

events.title: Maersk adjusts Far East-Bangladesh shipping routes
events.description: Maersk has revised its Far East–Bangladesh service network, including changes to the SH1, SH2, SH3, and IA7 services. The adjustments aim to optimize coverage and include the removal of calls at Hong Kong for the revised SH1 service.

events.title: Iran resumes Kharg Island oil loadings
events.description: Iran has resumed crude oil loading at its Kharg Island terminal after a six-week halt caused by a US-imposed blockade. The resumption follows the lifting of the US restrictions, impacting global oil supply chain operations.

events.title: CNC Line revises KCM2 service
events.description: CNC Line has revised its Korea–China–Malaysia (KCM2) service, removing calls at Shantou and the second Busan call, while adding Xiamen. The fleet has been reduced from seven to six vessels.

events.title: Maersk replaces ONE as Adriatic service vessel provider
events.description: Maersk will replace Ocean Network Express (ONE) as the vessel provider on the jointly operated Adriatic Service (AD1), which it will now market as the Adriatic Sea. ONE will remain as a slot charterer with limited port coverage.

events.title: US Monitors Hormuz Strait to Ensure Open Passage
events.description: The U.S. military is monitoring the Strait of Hormuz to ensure it remains open, countering Iran's claims that it had closed the critical shipping passage.

events.title: Americold opens cold chain hub at Port Saint John
events.description: Americold has opened a new integrated cold chain facility at Port Saint John in collaboration with DP World and Canadian Pacific Kansas City. The hub combines cold storage, port operations, and rail services to improve the efficiency of perishable freight movement.

events.title: Drone attack on Panama-flagged ship in Black Sea
events.description: A drone attack on a Panama-flagged ship in the Black Sea killed one crew member and injured two others, according to Panama's Maritime Authority.

events.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz to Shipping
events.description: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz for shipping due to what it described as a violation of a ceasefire. The move comes amid ongoing tensions and could severely disrupt global oil and freight flows.

events.title: Ships Allowed to Use South Hormuz Route with Signals On
events.description: Ships are now permitted to transit the southern route of the Strait of Hormuz at any time of day provided their signals are on, indicating a potential shift in navigation protocols in the critical chokepoint.

events.title: Allianz Wasserstraße launched to boost inland waterways
events.description: Rhenus Logistics, duisport, HGK Shipping, and Viking Cruises have formed the Allianz Wasserstraße alliance to strengthen inland waterways as a sustainable transport network for European supply chains.

events.title: Panama Canal conducts Gatun Locks maintenance
events.description: The Panama Canal is performing scheduled maintenance on a Gatun Locks chamber using a dry chamber operation. The maintenance is part of a long-term infrastructure program and is being carried out without disrupting transits.

events.title: MSC restructures intra-Far East shipping network
events.description: MSC is restructuring its intra-Far East network by discontinuing the Bayan Ko service and revising regional loops to maintain coverage between the Pearl River Delta, Vietnam, and the Philippines.

events.title: Iran establishes second unnamed chokepoint
events.description: Iran's Quds Force has constructed and tested a second chokepoint in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global shipping despite the strait reopening toll-free.
